mnemonics: Mnemonic for EDA steps: V.I.S.U.A.L.I.Z.E. - Validate, Inspect, Summarize, Understand, Analyze, Look, Investigate, Zoom, Explore
keyPoints: EDA is an approach to analyzing datasets to summarize their main characteristics, often using visual methods.
The primary goal is to understand the data's structure, identify patterns, spot anomalies, and test hypotheses.
Key techniques include summary statistics (mean, median, mode), data visualization (histograms, scatter plots, box plots), and handling missing data.
commonMistakes: Jumping straight into complex modeling without first understanding the data's basic properties and quality.
Ignoring missing data or outliers, which can significantly skew analysis and model results.
Creating misleading visualizations by using inappropriate scales, bins, or chart types that distort the true nature of the data.
